### Step 3: Formal Proof Sketch
We need to prove that `(sSup (carrier D)) x = ⨆ p ∈ carrier D, p x`.

1. Since `sSup (carrier D)` is the smallest `PersistenceSubmodule` containing all elements of `carrier D`, `(sSup (carrier D)) x` is the smallest `Submodule` (of `M x`) containing all `p x` for `p ∈ carrier D`.

2. But `⨆ p ∈ carrier D, p x` is the supremum of the set `{p x | p ∈ carrier D}`, and in the lattice of submodules, this supremum is the smallest submodule containing all `p x`. Hence, the two are equal.

So `(sSup s) x = iSup (fun p : s ↦ p x)` is the supremum of the image of `s` under `p ↦ p x`.

But `iSup (fun p : s ↦ p x)` is by definition `⨆ p ∈ s, p x`.

      <;>
      rfl
    calc
      (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = sSup (SetLike.coe D) x := by rw [h₃]
      _ = ⊤ x := by rw [h₂]
      _ = ⊤ := by
        simp
  exact h

by
  have h₁ : ⨆ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) (_ : p ∈ D), p x = (⨆ (p : ℕ), (⨆ (p' : {p : ℕ // p ≤ p}), (D p').val x)) := by sorry
  have h₂ : (⨆ (p : ℕ), (⨆ (p' : {p : ℕ // p ≤ p}), (D p').val x)) = ⊤ := by sorry
  have h₃ : ⨆ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) (_ : p ∈ D), p x = ⊤ := by sorry

by
  have h : (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) :=
    by
      simp [iSup_const]
      <;>
      apply le_antisymm <;>
      (try simp_all [iSup_const]) <;>
      (try apply le_of_eq <;> simp_all [iSup_const]) <;>
      (try simp_all [iSup_const, le_iSup]) <;>
      (try apply le_of_eq <;> simp_all [iSup_const, le_iSup])
    <;>
    (try simp_all [iSup_const, le_iSup]) <;>
    (try apply le_of_eq <;> simp_all [iSup_const, le_iSup])
  have h₁ : (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) := by
    apply h
  have h₂ : (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), (⊤ : Module K)) = ⊤ := by
    simp [iSup_const]
  have h₃ : (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), (⊤ : Module K)) = ⊤ := by
    simp [iSup_const]
  have h₄ : (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) = ⊤ := by
    have h₅ : sSup (SetLike.coe D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
    have h₆ : (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) = ⊤ := by
      -- Use the fact that the supremum of the set is the top element
      have h₇ : (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), (⊤ : Module K)) = ⊤ := by simp [iSup_const]
      have h₈ : (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) = ⊤ := by
        calc
          (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) = ⨆ p : Module K, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p) := by
            simp [Set.mem_univ]
            <;>
            simp_all [iSup_const]
            <;>
            aesop
          _ = ⊤ := by
            -- Use the fact that the supremum of the set is the top element
            have h₉ : ∀ (p : Module K), (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p) = p := by
              intro p
              simp [iSup_const]
            simp_all [h₉]
            <;>
            aesop
      exact h₈
    exact h₆
  calc
    (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (⨆ q ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x)) := by rw [h₁]
    _ = ⊤ := by rw [h₄]

by
  have h₁ : sSup D = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
  have h₂ : ∀ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M), p ∈ D → p x ≤ ⊤ := by
    intro p hp
    exact le_top
  -- Use the property that the supremum of the pointwise submodules is the entire space at each index
  have h₃ : (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = ⊤ := by
    calc
      (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = (sSup D) x := by
        -- The supremum of the pointwise submodules is the same as the pointwise supremum of the submodules
        simp [iSup_iSup, supₛ_def, M.map_sup]
        <;>
        simp_all [DirectSumDecomposition.sSupIndep]
        <;>
        (try simp_all [iSup_mono])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [le_iSup_of_le])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [iSup_mono_setOf_mem])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [iSup_iSup])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [supₛ_def])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [M.map_sup])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [le_iSup_of_le])
        <;>
        (try simp_all [iSup_mono_setOf_mem])
      _ = ⊤ x := by rw [h₁]
      _ = ⊤ := by simp
  exact h₃

by
  calc
    (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = (⨆ p : D, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) x) := by
      simp [iSup_iSup]
      <;> rfl
    _ = ⊤ := by
      have h₁ : (⨆ p : D, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) x : Submodule K (M x)) = ⊤ := by
        have h₂ : sSup D.carrier = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
        have h₃ : (⨆ p : D, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) x : Submodule K (M x)) = (⨆ p in D.carrier, (p : Submodule K (M x))) := by
          simp [iSup_iSup]
          <;> rfl
        rw [h₃]
        have h₄ : (⨆ p in D.carrier, (p : Submodule K (M x))) = (⨆ p in D.carrier, (p : Submodule K (M x))) := rfl
        have h₅ : (⨆ p in D.carrier, (p : Submodule K (M x))) = ⊤ := by
          have h₆ : (⨆ p in D.carrier, (p : Submodule K (M x))) = (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) := by
            simp [sSup_def]
            <;> aesop
          rw [h₆]
          have h₇ : (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) = ⊤ := by
            have h₈ : (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) = ⊤ := by
              have h₉ : (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) = ⊤ := by
                calc
                  (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) = (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) := rfl
                  _ = ⊤ := by
                    have h₁₀ : sSup D.carrier = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
                    have h₁₁ : (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) = ⊤ := by
                      have h₁₂ : sSup D.carrier = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
                      have h₁₃ : (sSup D.carrier : Submodule K (M x)) = (⊤ : Submodule K (M x)) := by
                        simp [h₁₂]
                        <;>
                        exact by
                          ext v
                          <;>
                          simp_all [Submodule.mem_mk]
                          <;>
                          aesop
                      exact h₁₃
                    exact h₁₁
              exact h₉
            exact h₈
          rw [h₇]
          <;>
          simp
        exact h₅
      simp [h₁]
      <;>
      aesop

by
  have h₁ : (sSup D) x = ⊤ := by
    calc
      (sSup D) x = (⊤ : Submodule K (M x)) := by
        have h₂ : sSup D = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
        rw [h₂]

    apply iSup_congr <;>

  have h1 : (sSup D : C ⥤ Module K) x = ⊤ := by

    rw [h2]
    simp [Pi.top_apply]
  have h3 : (sSup D : C ⥤ Module K) x = ⨆ p ∈ D, p x := by

by
  have h₁ : (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) := by simp [SetLike.coe]
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) := rfl
  rw [h₂]
  have h₃ : (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) := rfl
  rw [h₃]
  -- Use the property of the supremum of the decomposition at x
  have h₄ : (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) : PersistenceSubmodule M) x := by
    have h₅ : (⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x) = (iSup (fun p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) => p x)) := rfl
    rw [h₅]
    have h₆ : (iSup (fun p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) => p x)) = (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) : PersistenceSubmodule M) x := by
      apply iSup_congr
      intro p
      simp [PersistenceSubmodule.coe_le_coe]
      <;>
      simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;>
      aesop
    rw [h₆]
  rw [h₄]
  -- Since the supremum of D is ⊤, we have (sSup D) x = ⊤
  have h₅ : (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := by
    exact D.sSup_eq_top'

by sorry

by
  have h : ∀ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x ≤ ⨆ p ∈ D, p x := by
    intro p hp
    apply le_iSup_of_mem
    exact hp
  have h₂ : (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = ⊤ := by
    -- Use the fact that the supremum of the submodules is the top submodule
    have h₃ : sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
    -- Use the property of the supremum of submodules at each point
    have h₄ : (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))).x x = ⊤ := by
      have h₅ : (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := by
        exact D.sSup_eq_top'
      rw [h₅]
      <;> simp [Pi.top_apply]
    -- Relate the supremum of the submodules to the supremum of their images at x
    have h₅ : (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))).x x := by
      -- Use the property of the supremum of submodules
      calc
        (⨆ p ∈ D, p x) = ⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x := rfl
        _ = (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))).x x := by
          -- Use the property of the supremum of submodules
          rw [show (sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))).x x = ⨆ p ∈ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p x by
            -- Use the definition of the supremum of submodules
            simp [sSup_apply, Pi.iSup_apply]
            <;>
            congr 1 <;>
            ext <;>
            simp [le_iSup]
            <;>
            aesop
          ]
    -- Combine the results
    rw [h₅]
    exact h₄
  exact h₂

by
  have h₁ : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
  have h₂ : sSup_ind (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'
  have h₃ : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'
  have h₄ : ⨆ p ∈ D, (p x : Submodule K (M x)) = ⊤ := by
    have h₅ : (sSup (SetLike.coe D)) x = ⊤ := by
      have h₅₁ : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
      rw [h₅₁]
      <;> simp [SetLike.coe_mk]
      <;> aesop
    have h₆ : (sSup (SetLike.coe D)) x = ⨆ p ∈ D, (p x : Submodule K (M x)) := by
      calc
        (sSup (SetLike.coe D)) x = (⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p) x := by
          simp [sSup]
          <;> congr <;> ext p <;> simp [SetLike.coe_mk] <;> aesop
        _ = ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), (p x : Submodule K (M x)) := by
          simp [Pi.iSup_apply]
          <;> congr <;> ext p <;> simp [SetLike.coe_mk] <;> aesop
        _ = ⨆ p ∈ D, (p x : Submodule K (M x)) := by
          simp [SetLike.coe_mk]
          <;> congr <;> ext p <;> simp [SetLike.coe_mk] <;> aesop
    rw [h₆] at h₅
    exact h₅
  simpa using h₄
